summary: High‑energy 9/24/72 show with a monster 30‑minute Dark Star and solid soundboard tape despite minor cuts
review: The performance shines thanks to an epic Dark Star > China Cat Sunflower > The Rider sequence, tight Playing, soaring Bird Song and punchy Cumberland Blues.  The soundboard source is generally clear but suffers a few blips, muddy vocals and a short cut in Rider, yet the musical fire more than makes up for those blemishes.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is ~4.54; reviewers overwhelmingly praise the music (especially Dark Star) and consider the tape listenable despite minor technical flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mostly clear soundboard with occasional blips/cuts, slightly muddy vocals, a brief Rider cut and occasional tuning quirks—but overall well‑balanced mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls strong with Cumberland, Sugaree and Bird Song; the second set erupts in a 30‑minute Dark Star that flows into China Cat and Rider, delivering seamless medleys and high‑octane improvisation.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for perfect tone, expressive wah‑solo work and leading the jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is described as booming, thunderous and a highlight of the show, though one reviewer notes occasional out‑of‑tune moments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned delivering solid chords and adding texture; no major criticism.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s piano work praised for tasteful fills, acoustic‑piano/harpsichord vibe and supportive electric piano lines.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and occasionally Mickey) noted for tight, short‑sweet playing that drives the improvisations.
